languagePage.title: Choose the right language and learning direction
languagePage.subtitle: Each learner has a different goal. This page helps you choose Python, Web, JavaScript, C++ or Data/AI based on age, background and learning purpose.

coursePage.title: All courses
coursePage.subtitle: Courses are organized by foundation, projects, automation, web, algorithms and Data/AI. Learners should take an entry test before choosing a pace.
coursePage.noResult: No courses match the current filter.

courseDetail.lessonRoadmapDesc: Lessons progress from programming thinking and basic syntax to data handling and final mini projects.
courseDetail.plannedDesc: Detailed content will be opened later. Learners can start with the thinking test, placement test or Python Foundation first.

roadmapPage.title: Programming roadmap for each learner group
roadmapPage.subtitle: A roadmap is not just a list of courses. Each stage is designed around goals, age, thinking level, available study time and practical outcomes.

recommendationCard.title: Where should I start?
recommendationCard.desc: If you are unsure, take the thinking test first, then take the placement test for Python Foundation.

roadmapPage.whenToUse: When should you use this roadmap?
roadmapPage.stagesDesc: Each stage has clear goals and related courses.
roadmapPage.recommendedCoursesDesc: Courses are arranged from foundation to application.

python.name: Python
python.description: A readable and beginner-friendly language suitable for students, beginners, working adults, automation, data and AI.

javascript.name: JavaScript
javascript.description: The main language of the modern web, used for website interaction and as preparation for React.

web.name: Web Development
web.description: A path for building websites from HTML and CSS to JavaScript, React and complete frontend products.

cpp.name: C++
cpp.description: Suitable for algorithmic thinking, data structures, programming contests and high-performance problem solving.

dataAi.name: Data & AI
dataAi.description: A path for data processing, table analysis, data cleaning and introductory AI with Python.

pythonFoundation.title: Python Foundation
pythonFoundation.subtitle: Learn Python from zero
pythonFoundation.description: A foundation course that helps learners understand programming thinking, write basic Python programs, use conditions, loops, functions, lists, files and complete mini projects.
pythonFoundation.target: Grades 6-12 students, absolute beginners, learners with weak foundations and working adults who want a structured Python start.
outcomes: Use variables, data types, conditions and loops
prerequisites: Thinking Test or Placement Test is recommended
tools: VS Code or online Python runner

pythonProjects.title: Python Mini Projects
pythonProjects.subtitle: Practice Python through small projects
pythonProjects.description: A practice course that reinforces Python through practical console projects such as quizzes, games, list management and file processing.
pythonProjects.target: Learners who completed Python Foundation or already know variables, conditions, loops, lists and functions.

pythonAutomation.title: Python Automation for Work
pythonAutomation.subtitle: Automate work with Python
pythonAutomation.description: Use Python to handle files, table data, repeated reports, batch file renaming and office workflows.
pythonAutomation.target: Working adults, office staff, technicians and anyone who wants to save time with automation.
prerequisites: A real workflow to automate is helpful

webFoundation.title: Web Foundation
webFoundation.subtitle: HTML, CSS and website thinking
webFoundation.description: An entry web course covering page structure, layout, responsiveness and the first landing page.
webFoundation.target: Learners starting web development or preparing for JavaScript/React.

javascriptFoundation.title: JavaScript Foundation
javascriptFoundation.subtitle: JavaScript basics for the web
javascriptFoundation.description: A JavaScript foundation course covering variables, conditions, loops, arrays, objects, functions and basic DOM interaction.
javascriptFoundation.target: Learners who want to study web/frontend or prepare for React.

reactFoundation.title: React Foundation
reactFoundation.subtitle: Build modern web interfaces
reactFoundation.description: Learn React components, props, state, events, routing and build small web apps with clear structure.
reactFoundation.target: Learners who know basic JavaScript and want modern frontend development.

cppFoundation.title: C++ Foundation
cppFoundation.subtitle: C++ basics for algorithmic thinking
cppFoundation.description: A C++ foundation course for students who want strict syntax, algorithm practice and basic programming problem solving.
cppFoundation.target: Students with basic math logic who want algorithms or programming contests.

algorithmBasic.title: Algorithm Basic
algorithmBasic.subtitle: Algorithmic thinking and problem solving
algorithmBasic.description: Practice problem analysis, pattern finding, simple optimization and introductory data structures.
algorithmBasic.target: Students or learners who want stronger algorithmic thinking before advanced topics.

dataFoundation.title: Data Foundation with Python
dataFoundation.subtitle: Data processing basics
dataFoundation.description: Learn table-shaped data, CSV/Excel reading and writing, basic data cleaning and simple reporting with Python.
dataFoundation.target: Working adults, students and learners preparing for Data/AI.

aiBeginner.title: AI Beginner
aiBeginner.subtitle: A practical introduction to AI
aiBeginner.description: Understand training data, models, prediction, evaluation and how to build a simple AI demo.
aiBeginner.target: Learners who know Python and want a practical introduction to AI.

student.title: Roadmap for grades 6-12
student.shortDesc: From logical thinking to Python, projects and algorithms.
student.description: This roadmap prioritizes thinking, confidence and problem-solving habits. Younger learners should start visually and with daily-life examples, then move into Python and projects.
student.whenToUse: Use this for students who need a structured path and for parents who want to know whether the child should start with thinking, Python or algorithms.

stages.title: Thinking assessment
stages.description: Take the Thinking Test to understand pattern recognition, logic, data reasoning and debugging mindset.
items: Separate fun learning, foundation learning and contest preparation

stages.title: Python Foundation
stages.description: Learn Python from input/output, variables, conditions and loops to functions and mini projects.
items: Each lesson has quiz and practice

stages.title: Projects and self-learning habits
stages.description: Practice small projects so learners understand how to break down problems and finish products.

stages.title: Advanced branch
stages.description: If the learner enjoys problem solving and has strong math foundations, move to C++ and algorithms.

beginner.title: Roadmap for absolute beginners
beginner.description: This roadmap is for learners who have never coded before. The goal is to understand how computers process data, write small programs and choose the next direction after the foundation.
beginner.whenToUse: Use this when the learner is unsure whether to learn Python, Web or JavaScript and needs a low-pressure path.

stages.title: Thinking warm-up
stages.description: Start with thinking tests and daily-life examples to understand algorithms as step-by-step instructions.

stages.title: Python Foundation
stages.description: Python is a safe first choice because the syntax is readable and beginner-friendly.

stages.title: Project practice
stages.description: After the foundation, learners should build small projects to move from theory to doing.

stages.title: Choose the next direction
stages.description: If the learner likes interfaces, move to Web/JavaScript. If the learner likes data, continue to Data Foundation.

workingAdult.title: Roadmap for working adults
workingAdult.shortDesc: Application-first: automation, data and time saving.
workingAdult.description: This roadmap prioritizes practical usage: files, table data, repeated reports and office automation. Learners do not need deep algorithms at the start.
workingAdult.whenToUse: Use this for working adults who want programming for real workflows, reports, data or repeated tasks.

stages.title: Identify work problems
stages.description: Before going deep, identify what to automate: files, reports, data, email or error checking.

stages.title: Python Foundation
stages.description: Learn the Python parts needed for logic, data and functions.

stages.title: Automation for Work
stages.description: Apply Python to files, folders, CSV/Excel and reports.

stages.title: Data and intro AI
stages.description: If work involves data, continue to Data Foundation and AI Beginner.

careerSwitcher.title: Roadmap for career switchers
careerSwitcher.shortDesc: From programming basics to web, portfolio projects and data.
careerSwitcher.description: This path needs clear career goals. Learners should build a Python or JavaScript foundation, create projects, read documentation and build an initial portfolio.
careerSwitcher.whenToUse: Use this for learners who want to move into developer, automation/data roles or need a programming foundation for tech careers.
goals: Prepare for frontend or data paths

stages.title: Assess the foundation
stages.description: Take the Thinking Test and Placement Test to decide whether to start from Python Foundation or move faster.

stages.title: Programming foundation
stages.description: Python Foundation builds code thinking, functions, data and projects before choosing a career branch.

stages.title: Web/frontend branch
stages.description: For interface-oriented learners, study Web Foundation, JavaScript and React.

stages.title: Data/automation branch
stages.description: For data or automation paths, study Python Automation and Data Foundation.
